title: 13 Best Herbal Teas For Insomnia
intro: Herbal teas for insomnia are a type of herbal remedy made from various plants and flowers that help individuals with difficulty falling or staying asleep. They can effectively treat insomnia due to their calming and soothing properties, which help to relax the mind and body. Examples of herbal teas that can help with insomnia include chamomile tea, which has a sedative effect and calms the nervous system; lavender tea, which promotes relaxation and reduces anxiety; valerian root tea, which has a sedative effect and improves sleep quality; passionflower tea, which reduces anxiety and promotes a restful sleep; and lemon balm tea, which has a calming effect and helps to reduce stress. These teas work by interacting with the body's natural sleep-wake cycle and promoting a restful and rejuvenating sleep.

preparations.herb_desc: Valeriana officinalis teas have been a long-standing remedy for treating insomnia, a common sleep disorder characterized by difficulty falling or staying asleep. This herbal preparation contains a range of bioactive constituents, including valerenic acid, isovaltrate, and valepotriates, which have been shown to possess sedative and anxiolytic properties that help to calm the mind and body, promoting relaxation and reducing stress. The sedative properties of Valeriana officinalis teas work by increasing the activity of gamma-aminobutyric acid (GABA), a neurotransmitter that helps to regulate sleep and relaxation, thereby helping to treat insomnia. The benefits of using Valeriana officinalis teas to treat insomnia include improved sleep quality, reduced anxiety and stress levels, and a natural and non-habit-forming approach to managing this common sleep disorder.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of boiling water and 1 teaspoon of dried Valeriana officinalis root in a cup or teapot.
Steep the Valeriana officinalis root in the boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into another cup.
Add honey or sugar to taste, if desired, to sweeten the tea.
Drink the tea 30 minutes to 1 hour before bedtime to help with sleep and relaxation.
preparations.preparation_study: According to the study, Valeriana officinalis teas for insomnia may be associated with improvements in some sleep parameters, such as sleep latency and quality of sleep, when used on its own or in combination with hops.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Valeriana officinalis teas can cause drowsiness, dizziness, and stomach upset in some individuals when used to treat insomnia, especially if consumed in large quantities or combined with other sedatives. To use Valeriana officinalis teas safely, it is recommended to start with small doses, avoid consuming it before driving or operating heavy machinery, and not to use it for extended periods as it may lead to dependency.
preparation_usage: Consult a doctor before using Valeriana officinalis for insomnia
Start with 1 teaspoon of dried roots in a cup of boiling water
Drink 1 cup of tea 30 minutes before bedtime
Use 2-3 times a week, as needed, for up to 2 weeks
Monitor sleep quality and adjust dosage or frequency as needed

preparations.herb_desc: Passiflora incarnata teas have been traditionally used to treat insomnia due to their calming and sedative properties. The herbal preparation helps to treat insomnia by promoting relaxation, reducing anxiety, and regulating sleep patterns. The bioactive constituents of Passiflora incarnata, including flavonoids, alkaloids, and glycosides, are responsible for its sedative effects, particularly harmine, which helps to induce sleep and reduce restlessness. The benefits of using Passiflora incarnata teas for insomnia include improved sleep quality, reduced symptoms of anxiety and stress, and a natural approach to managing sleep disorders.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh or dried Passiflora incarnata flowers. If using fresh flowers, dry them in a low-temperature oven (150°F) for 1-2 hours.
Measure 1 tablespoon of dried flowers (or 2 tablespoons of fresh flowers) and place them in a heat-resistant cup or infuser.
Heat 1 cup of water to a boil and pour it over the flowers in the cup.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes, then strain the liquid into another cup using a strainer or cheesecloth.
Drink 1/2 to 1 cup of the tea 30 minutes before bedtime to help with insomnia. You can repeat as needed, but consult with a doctor before making it a regular habit.
preparations.preparation_study: According to the study, Passiflora incarnata teas for insomnia showed potential as one of the three most effective medicinal plants, along with valerian and ashwagandha, particularly when combined with other herbs.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Passiflora incarnata teas can be an effective herbal remedy for insomnia, but it may also cause drowsiness, dizziness, and nausea in some individuals due to its sedative properties. To use Passiflora incarnata teas safely, it is recommended to start with a low dose and gradually increase as needed, and to avoid driving or operating heavy machinery until the effects of the tea are well understood.
preparation_usage: Consult a doctor before using Passiflora incarnata tea for insomnia.
Start with 1 teaspoon of dried Passiflora incarnata per cup of boiling water.
Steep for 5-10 minutes, then strain the tea.
Drink 1 cup of Passiflora incarnata tea 30 minutes before bedtime.
Use 2-3 times a week, with a maximum of 3 cups per day.

preparation_amazon.title: Traditional Medicinals Organic Nighty Night Tea, 16 Tea Bags (Pack of 1)

preparations.herb_desc: Lavandula angustifolia teas have been traditionally used as a natural remedy to treat insomnia, a common sleep disorder characterized by difficulty falling or staying asleep. The sedative properties of this herbal preparation help to treat insomnia by promoting relaxation, reducing anxiety, and regulating sleep patterns. The bioactive constituents of Lavandula angustifolia, including linalool and linalyl acetate, have been shown to interact with the body's GABA receptors, producing a calming effect that facilitates a restful night's sleep. The benefits of using Lavandula angustifolia teas to treat insomnia include improved sleep quality, reduced symptoms of anxiety and depression, and a natural, non-habit-forming solution to promote better sleep hygiene.
preparation_procedure: Gather 2 tablespoons of dried Lavandula angustifolia flowers.
Boil 1 cup of water in a pot.
Remove the pot from heat and add the dried Lavandula angustifolia flowers.
Steep the flowers for 5-7 minutes, then strain the liquid.
Drink the tea before bedtime to help with insomnia.
preparations.preparation_study: According to the Journal of Psychopharmacology (Oxford, England), Lavandula angustifolia teas for insomnia may be an effective option when used as part of an overall approach to creating a tranquil ambience, especially when combined with valerian, as the combination of the two was even more effective for the control of insomnia in a personal trial.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Lavandula angustifolia teas can help alleviate insomnia symptoms, but potential side effects include dizziness, headaches, and allergic reactions in some individuals, particularly those with sensitive skin or respiratory issues. To use Lavandula angustifolia teas safely, take precautions by drinking them in moderation, avoiding consumption if pregnant or breastfeeding, and keeping them out of reach of children to minimize potential risks.
preparation_usage: Drink 1 cup of Lavandula angustifolia tea 30 minutes before bedtime.
Start with 1/2 teaspoon of dried Lavandula per cup of boiling water.
Gradually increase to 1 teaspoon per cup if insomnia persists.
Use 2-3 times a week for maintenance and as needed for insomnia.
Consult a healthcare professional for personalized dosage and advice.

preparations.herb_desc: Melissa officinalis teas, also known as lemon balm tea, have been traditionally used to treat insomnia due to its calming and sedative properties. The herbal preparation helps to treat insomnia by promoting relaxation and reducing stress levels, making it easier to fall asleep and maintain a restful sleep. The bioactive constituents of Melissa officinalis, including rosmarinic acid, apigenin, and luteolin, have been found to have a sedative effect on the nervous system, contributing to its sleep-promoting properties. The benefits of Melissa officinalis teas in treating insomnia include improved sleep quality, reduced anxiety and stress levels, and enhanced overall well-being.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of Melissa officinalis leaves, 1 cup of boiling water, and a tea infuser.
Measure 2 tablespoons of honey (optional) and set aside.
Steep the Melissa officinalis leaves in boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup using the tea infuser and discard the leaves.
Add 2 tablespoons of honey (if using) and stir well before drinking.
preparations.preparation_study: According to the study, Melissa officinalis teas, which is lemon balm extract, in combination with melatonin and vitamin B6, showed a statistically significant improvement in sleep quality and other sleep-related parameters in patients with mild-to-moderate insomnia.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Melissa officinalis teas can be beneficial in treating insomnia, but they may cause side effects such as stomach upset, nausea, and dizziness due to the high concentration of essential oils and volatile compounds. To minimize potential risks, it is recommended to start with low doses, gradually increase the amount, and avoid using Melissa officinalis teas with other sedatives or stimulants, and to limit their consumption to short-term periods, typically up to two weeks.
preparation_usage: Drink 1-2 cups of Melissa officinalis tea 30 minutes before bedtime.
Start with 1 teaspoon of dried Melissa officinalis per cup of boiling water.
Gradually increase dosage to 2 teaspoons per cup if needed.
Consume Melissa officinalis tea 2-3 times a week for optimal results.
Combine with a relaxing bedtime routine for better sleep quality.

preparations.herb_desc: Avena sativa teas, also known as oat straw tea, have been traditionally used to treat insomnia due to their sedative and calming properties. The herbal preparation helps to treat insomnia by promoting relaxation, reducing anxiety, and regulating sleep patterns, making it easier to fall asleep and stay asleep throughout the night. The bioactive constituents of Avena sativa tea, including avenacosides and avenalumic acid, have been found to have a sedative effect on the nervous system, which contributes to its sleep-promoting properties. The benefits of using Avena sativa teas to treat insomnia include improved sleep quality, reduced stress and anxiety, and a sense of relaxation and calmness, making it a natural and holistic approach to managing insomnia.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of Avena sativa (oat straw) and 1 cup of boiling water.
Measure 1 tablespoon of dried Avena sativa and add it to a tea infuser or a heat-resistant cup.
Pour 1 cup of boiling water over the Avena sativa and let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into another cup using the tea infuser or a fine-mesh sieve.
Drink 1 cup of the Avena sativa tea 30 minutes before bedtime to help with insomnia.
preparations.preparation_study: According to "The Journal of nervous and mental disease", Avena sativa teas for insomnia have been identified as a potential natural remedy due to their bioactive metabolites which have not been well reported to have side effects.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Avena sativa teas can be used to treat insomnia, but potential side effects may include dizziness, headaches, and stomach upset in some individuals, particularly those with pre-existing conditions or allergies. To use Avena sativa teas safely, take them in moderation, avoid mixing them with other sedatives or medications, and be aware of the potential interactions with other herbal remedies, as well as monitor your body's response and adjust usage accordingly.
preparation_usage: Consult doctor before using Avena sativa tea for insomnia
Start with 1 teaspoon of Avena sativa powder in 1 cup of boiling water
Drink 1 cup of tea 30 minutes before bedtime, 2-3 times a week
Gradually increase dosage to 1-2 teaspoons in 1 cup of water
Monitor sleep patterns and adjust dosage as needed

preparations.herb_desc: Ginkgo biloba teas have been traditionally used to treat insomnia due to their sedative and calming properties, which help to promote relaxation and reduce stress levels. The bioactive constituents of Ginkgo biloba, such as flavonoids and terpenoids, are believed to interact with the brain's neurotransmitters, including serotonin and GABA, to regulate sleep patterns and induce a state of calmness. By enhancing sleep quality and duration, Ginkgo biloba teas can help alleviate insomnia symptoms and improve overall sleep hygiene. The benefits of using Ginkgo biloba teas to treat insomnia include improved sleep quality, reduced stress and anxiety, and a sense of relaxation and calmness, making it a popular natural remedy for this common sleep disorder.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of boiling water and 1 teaspoon of dried Ginkgo biloba leaves.
Measure 1 tablespoon of dried Ginkgo biloba leaves and place them in a tea infuser or a small piece of cheesecloth.
Steep the Ginkgo biloba leaves in the boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup and discard the leaves.
Drink the tea 30 minutes before bedtime to help with insomnia.
preparations.preparation_study: According to "Pharmacopsychiatry", Ginkgo biloba teas for insomnia may improve sleep continuity and enhance Non-REM sleep, potentially due to a weakening of tonic CRH-activity, suggesting a potential new treatment strategy for the depressive syndrome with sleep disturbance.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Ginkgo biloba teas can be beneficial in treating insomnia, but possible side effects may include dizziness, headaches, and stomach upset due to its interaction with certain medications or exacerbating existing conditions like bleeding disorders. Precautions to take when using Ginkgo biloba teas to treat insomnia include avoiding its use with sedatives, antacids, and blood thinners, and being cautious if you have a history of seizures, high blood pressure, or are pregnant or breastfeeding.
preparation_usage: Drink 1/2 cup of Ginkgo biloba tea 30 minutes before bedtime.
Start with 1 teaspoon of dried Ginkgo biloba leaves per cup.
Gradually increase dose to 1 1/2 teaspoons if needed.
Use 1-2 cups of Ginkgo biloba tea per day for insomnia relief.
Continue using Ginkgo biloba tea for 2-4 weeks for best results.

preparations.herb_desc: Zingiber officinale teas, also known as ginger tea, have been traditionally used to treat insomnia due to its calming and sedative properties. The bioactive constituents of ginger tea, including gingerols and shogaols, help to reduce inflammation and promote relaxation, which can aid in falling asleep. The benefits of ginger tea in treating insomnia include improved sleep quality, reduced anxiety and stress levels, and enhanced mood regulation. By consuming ginger tea before bedtime, individuals can take advantage of its natural sleep-promoting properties to alleviate insomnia symptoms and improve overall sleep health.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of boiling water, 1 tablespoon of dried Zingiber officinale root, and a tea infuser or strainer.
Measure 1 tablespoon of dried Zingiber officinale root and place it in the tea infuser or strainer.
Pour 1 cup of boiling water over the Zingiber officinale root in the infuser or strainer.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes, then remove the infuser or strainer.
Drink the tea before bedtime to help with insomnia. Store any leftover tea in the refrigerator for up to 24 hours.
preparations.preparation_study: N/A
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Zingiber officinale teas can be used to treat insomnia, but possible side effects may include stomach upset, nausea, and diarrhea due to its digestive stimulating properties. To minimize potential side effects, take Zingiber officinale teas in moderation and avoid consuming it on an empty stomach, as this may exacerbate digestive issues, and also consider avoiding it in the evening to ensure a restful night's sleep.
preparation_usage: Consult a doctor before using Zingiber officinale tea for insomnia
Start with 1/2 teaspoon of dried root powder per cup of boiling water
Steep for 5-7 minutes, then strain and discard solids
Drink 1 cup 30 minutes before bedtime, 2 times a week, as needed
Gradually increase dose to 1 teaspoon per cup if insomnia persists

preparations.herb_desc: Humulus lupulus teas, also known as hops tea, have been used to treat insomnia due to their sedative properties, which help to calm the mind and body, promoting a restful night's sleep. The bioactive constituents, including flavonoids, terpenoids, and essential oils, contribute to its sedative effects by interacting with the brain's GABA receptors and reducing anxiety. The benefits of hops tea in treating insomnia include improved sleep quality, reduced symptoms of anxiety and stress, and enhanced relaxation, making it an effective natural remedy for individuals struggling with sleep disorders. Regular consumption of hops tea can also lead to improved overall well-being and a reduced risk of developing chronic sleep-related issues.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Humulus lupulus flowers or 1 teaspoon of dried flowers.
Heat 1 cup of water in a pot until it boils.
Remove the pot from the heat and add the Humulus lupulus flowers.
Steep the flowers for 5-7 minutes, then strain the mixture.
Drink the tea before bedtime to help with insomnia.
preparations.preparation_study: According to the study, Humulus lupulus teas, often used in combination with valerian, may be associated with improvements in sleep parameters such as sleep latency and quality of sleep in adults with primary insomnia.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Humulus lupulus teas can be an effective herbal remedy for treating insomnia, but it may also cause side effects such as dizziness, headaches, and stomach upset in some individuals. To minimize potential risks, individuals should start with small amounts, be cautious when consuming it with other sedatives or medications, and avoid driving or operating heavy machinery after consuming the tea.
preparation_usage: Consult a doctor before using Humulus lupulus teas for insomnia.
Start with 1 teaspoon (tsp) of dried Humulus lupulus per cup of hot water.
Drink 1 cup of Humulus lupulus tea 30 minutes before bedtime.
Use 1-2 cups of Humulus lupulus tea per day, as needed, for 7-14 days.
Monitor sleep patterns and adjust dosage or frequency as recommended by your doctor.

preparations.herb_desc: Scutellaria baicalensis teas, also known as Chinese skullcap tea, have been used for centuries to treat insomnia due to its sedative and calming properties. This herbal preparation helps to treat insomnia by promoting relaxation, reducing anxiety and stress levels, and regulating the sleep-wake cycle. The bioactive constituents present in Scutellaria baicalensis tea, such as baicalein and baicalin, possess flavonoid and coumarin properties that contribute to its sedative effects, helping to induce a restful and uninterrupted sleep. The benefits of using Scutellaria baicalensis tea to treat insomnia include improved sleep quality, reduced sleep latency, and enhanced overall well-being, making it a natural and effective remedy for individuals struggling with this common health issue.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Scutellaria baicalensis root and 1 cup of boiling water.
Measure 2 tablespoons of dried Scutellaria baicalensis root into a tea infuser or a heat-resistant cup.
Pour 1 cup of boiling water over the dried root.
Let it steep for 5-10 minutes. Strain the tea before drinking.
Drink the tea 30 minutes before bedtime to help with insomnia.
preparations.preparation_study: According to "Science bulletin", Scutellaria baicalensis teas for insomnia may be beneficial due to the presence of flavones such as baicalin, wogonoside, and their aglycones baicalein and wogonin, which have been reported to have neuroprotective effects.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Scutellaria baicalensis teas can be used to treat insomnia, but they may cause side effects such as dizziness, nausea, and stomach upset due to their strong adaptogenic properties. Precautions should be taken when using Scutellaria baicalensis teas for insomnia treatment, including avoiding use during pregnancy and breastfeeding, as well as being cautious when driving or operating heavy machinery due to potential drowsiness.
preparation_usage: Consult doctor before using Scutellaria baicalensis teas for insomnia treatment.
Start with 1 teaspoon of dried herb per 8 oz of water, 30 minutes before bedtime.
Drink 1 cup of Scutellaria baicalensis tea, 2-3 times a week, as needed.
Increase dosage to 2 teaspoons per 8 oz of water if insomnia persists, after doctor's approval.
Monitor sleep patterns and adjust dosage or consult doctor as needed.

preparations.herb_desc: Cannabis sativa teas have been traditionally used to treat insomnia due to their calming and sedative properties, which help to relax the mind and body, making it easier to fall asleep. The bioactive constituents of Cannabis sativa, including cannabidiol (CBD) and terpenes, play a crucial role in treating insomnia by interacting with the body's endocannabinoid system and promoting a sense of relaxation and reduced anxiety. By consuming Cannabis sativa teas, individuals can experience a reduction in stress and anxiety levels, leading to improved sleep quality and duration. The benefits of using Cannabis sativa teas to treat insomnia include increased relaxation, reduced stress and anxiety, and improved sleep patterns, making it a popular natural remedy for those struggling with this common sleep disorder.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of boiling water and 1-2 teaspoons of dried Cannabis sativa flowers.
Steep the flowers in the boiling water for 5-7 minutes, then strain the mixture into a cup.
Add 1 tablespoon of honey to the tea to sweeten it, if desired.
Let the tea cool down for 2-3 minutes before consumption.
Drink the tea 30 minutes before bedtime to help with insomnia.
preparations.preparation_study: According to "Journal of advanced pharmaceutical technology & research", Cannabis sativa teas for insomnia may have sedative-like effects due to their modulation of GABAergic neurons and induction of the cannabinoid CB1 receptor mechanism.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Cannabis sativa teas can be an effective remedy for treating insomnia, but using it may cause side effects such as dry mouth, dizziness, and increased heart rate. To use Cannabis sativa teas safely, it's essential to start with low doses and gradually increase the amount as needed, be cautious when consuming before driving or operating heavy machinery, and avoid using it with other sleep aids or sedatives.
preparation_usage: Consult a doctor before using Cannabis sativa teas for insomnia.
Start with a small dose of 1/4 teaspoon per cup of tea, 30 minutes before bed.
Gradually increase the dose to 1/2 teaspoon per cup if needed, but not exceeding 1 teaspoon.
Use the tea 2-3 times a week, not daily, to avoid habituation and dependency.
Monitor your sleep patterns and adjust the dose or frequency as needed, under doctor's guidance.

preparations.herb_desc: Silybum marianum teas, also known as milk thistle tea, have been traditionally used to treat insomnia due to their sedative properties. This herbal preparation helps to treat insomnia by promoting relaxation and reducing stress levels, making it easier to fall asleep and stay asleep. The bioactive constituents of Silybum marianum, including flavonoids and silymarin, have been found to have a calming effect on the nervous system, which contributes to its sleep-promoting properties. The benefits of using Silybum marianum teas to treat insomnia include improved sleep quality, reduced anxiety, and a sense of relaxation and calmness.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Silybum marianum flowers and leaves, and a tea infuser or a piece of cheesecloth.
Boil 1 cup of water in a pot and let it cool for 1 minute.
Place the Silybum marianum flowers and leaves in the tea infuser or cheesecloth, and steep in the boiled water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup and discard the Silybum marianum flowers and leaves.
Drink 1 cup of the tea 30 minutes before bedtime, and avoid consuming it during the day.
preparations.preparation_study: N/A
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Silybum marianum teas can be effective in treating insomnia due to their sedative properties, but possible side effects include dizziness, nausea, and stomach upset when consumed in excess. Precautions should be taken to avoid interactions with other medications and to limit consumption to recommended doses to minimize the risk of side effects such as allergic reactions and liver damage.
preparation_usage: Drink 1 cup of Silybum marianum tea 30 minutes before bedtime.
Start with 1 teaspoon of dried herb per cup of boiling water.
Gradually increase to 2 teaspoons per cup if needed.
Use 1-2 cups per day, with 1 cup at bedtime.
Continue usage for 7-10 days before reassessing.

Drink 1 cup of the tea 30 minutes before bedtime.

preparation_usage: Drink 1 cup of Lavandula latifolia tea 30 minutes before bedtime.
Start with 1 teaspoon of dried herb per cup of boiling water.
Gradually increase dosage to 2 teaspoons per cup if needed.
Use 2-3 times a week to start, then daily if needed.

The bioactive constituents, including flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, contribute to its sleep-promoting effects by modulating the hormonal balance and reducing stress levels. The herbal preparation helps to regulate the body's circadian rhythms and promote a restful sleep by interacting with the neurotransmitters and hormones involved in sleep regulation. The benefits of consuming Vitex agnus-castus teas for insomnia include improved sleep quality, reduced symptoms of anxiety and stress, and a sense of overall well-being.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 teaspoon of dried Vitex agnus-castus flowers and a cup of boiling water.
Pour the boiling water over the dried flowers in a cup or teapot.
Let the mixture steep for 5-7 minutes to allow the flavors to release.
Strain the mixture into another cup using a fine-mesh sieve or a piece of cheesecloth.
Drink the tea 30 minutes before bedtime to help with insomnia.

preparations.herb_desc: Camellia sinensis teas, derived from the leaves of the Camellia sinensis plant, have been traditionally used to treat insomnia due to their calming and sedative properties. The flavonoids, particularly quercetin and kaempferol, present in these teas, help to promote relaxation and reduce anxiety, thereby aiding in a restful sleep. The L-theanine content in Camellia sinensis teas also contributes to its sleep-promoting effects by regulating the body's sleep-wake cycle and inducing a state of calmness. Regular consumption of Camellia sinensis teas has been shown to improve sleep quality, reduce stress and anxiety, and promote overall well-being, making it a beneficial herbal preparation for treating insomnia.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 teaspoon of Camellia sinensis leaves (oolong or green tea). Choose a high-quality tea with minimal processing.
Heat 1 cup of water to 200°F (93°C) in a kettle or pot. Use filtered water to prevent mineral buildup.
Steep the tea leaves in the hot water for 2-3 minutes. You can use a tea infuser or a strainer to hold the leaves.
Strain the tea and discard the leaves. Add 1/2 teaspoon of honey (optional) to help with sleep.
Drink 1 cup of the tea 30 minutes before bedtime. Camellia sinensis teas can help promote relaxation and improve sleep quality.
preparations.preparation_study: According to Disease-a-month : DM, Camellia sinensis teas, more commonly known as tea from the Camellia sinensis plant, was not mentioned in the provided study for its effect on insomnia, however, Valerian was found beneficial for insomnia.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Camellia sinensis teas can potentially induce side effects such as dizziness, stomach upset, and interact with medications due to their high caffeine content when used to treat insomnia. To safely use these teas, it's essential to start with a low dose, avoid consuming them in the afternoon or evening, and be cautious when combining them with other stimulants or sedatives.

preparations.herb_desc: Artemisia absinthium teas have been traditionally used to treat insomnia due to their sedative and calming properties, which help to soothe the mind and body. The herbal preparation contains bioactive constituents such as absinthin, thujone, and sesquiterpenes, which have been shown to have a relaxing effect on the nervous system, promoting a restful night's sleep. By reducing stress and anxiety, Artemisia absinthium teas help to treat insomnia by regulating sleep patterns and improving the quality of sleep. The benefits of this herbal preparation include improved sleep duration, reduced sleep latency, and enhanced overall well-being, making it a natural and effective remedy for insomnia sufferers.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 teaspoon of dried Artemisia absinthium leaves.
Heat 1 cup of water in a pot.
Steep the dried leaves in the hot water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup and discard the leaves.
Drink the tea 30 minutes before bedtime to help with insomnia.
preparations.preparation_study: According to Neurology research international, Artemisia absinthium teas for insomnia have shown hypnotic activity, increasing sleep duration and decreasing sleep latency in mice, particularly at doses of 100 and 200 mg/kg.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Artemisia absinthium teas can be used to treat insomnia, but they may cause side effects such as dizziness, headaches, and increased heart rate due to the presence of thujone, a toxic compound that can be detrimental to the central nervous system. Precautions should be taken to avoid consuming large quantities, especially when combined with other sedatives or stimulants, as this can exacerbate the risk of adverse reactions and lead to dependence on the tea.

preparations.herb_desc: Cymbopogon citratus teas, also known as lemongrass tea, have been traditionally used to treat insomnia due to their calming and soothing properties. The bioactive constituents present in this herbal preparation, including citral and limonene, help to regulate the sleep-wake cycle and promote relaxation, thereby alleviating symptoms of insomnia. By reducing stress and anxiety, Cymbopogon citratus teas create a conducive environment for a restful sleep, allowing individuals to overcome their insomnia issues. The benefits of using Cymbopogon citratus teas to treat insomnia include improved sleep quality, reduced sleep latency, and enhanced overall well-being.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Cymbopogon citratus leaves and 1 cup of boiling water.
Chop the Cymbopogon citratus leaves into small pieces and add them to a tea infuser.
Steep the leaves in the boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup and add 1 tablespoon of honey to taste.
Drink the tea 30 minutes before bedtime to help with insomnia.
preparations.preparation_study: According to Psychosomatic medicine, Cymbopogon citratus teas are not mentioned in the provided study as a treatment for insomnia, however, valerian is mentioned as a natural product that has demonstrable effects on insomnia.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Cymbopogon citratus teas can potentially alleviate insomnia symptoms, but its use may be associated with side effects such as stomach upset, nausea, and dizziness in some individuals. To minimize risks, it is recommended to start with small doses, avoid consuming it before bedtime, and be cautious when taking it with other medications or substances that may interact with its active compounds.

preparations.herb_desc: Piper methysticum teas, also known as kava teas, have been traditionally used to treat insomnia due to their sedative and anxiolytic properties. The bioactive constituents of kava, including kavalactones and flavokavains, help to regulate the sleep-wake cycle by interacting with the body's GABA receptors, promoting relaxation and reducing stress levels. Drinking kava tea before bedtime can lead to improved sleep quality, increased relaxation, and reduced anxiety, making it an effective natural remedy for insomnia sufferers. Regular consumption of kava tea has been associated with various benefits, including improved sleep duration, reduced symptoms of insomnia, and enhanced overall well-being.
preparation_procedure: Get 1 teaspoon of dried Piper methysticum root powder.
Boil 1 cup of water in a pot.
Add the root powder to the boiling water and let it simmer for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup and discard the solids.
Drink the tea 30 minutes to 1 hour before bedtime.
preparations.preparation_study: According to Journal of clinical psychopharmacology, Piper methysticum teas are not specifically mentioned for insomnia in the provided study, as it primarily focuses on the efficacy of kava extract as a symptomatic treatment for anxiety.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Piper methysticum teas can be effective in treating insomnia, but they may cause side effects such as dizziness, nausea, and headaches due to its sedative properties. When using Piper methysticum teas for insomnia, it's essential to take precautions, such as starting with low doses, being cautious with alcohol and other sedatives, and avoiding driving or operating heavy machinery, especially in the morning after consumption.

preparations.herb_desc: Lavandula stoechas teas have been traditionally used to treat insomnia, a common sleep disorder characterized by difficulty falling or staying asleep. The properties of this herbal preparation, including its calming and sedative effects, help to treat insomnia by promoting relaxation and reducing anxiety. The bioactive constituents of Lavandula stoechas teas, such as linalool and linalyl acetate, are responsible for its anxiolytic and hypnotic properties, which help to induce a restful night's sleep. The benefits of using Lavandula stoechas teas to treat insomnia include improved sleep quality, reduced stress and anxiety, and a sense of calm and relaxation, making it a natural and effective remedy for this common sleep disorder.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 tablespoon of dried Lavandula stoechas flowers.
Heat 1 cup of water until it boils.
Add the dried Lavandula stoechas flowers to the boiling water.
Let the mixture steep for 5-7 minutes, then strain it.
Drink the tea before bedtime to help with insomnia.
preparations.preparation_study: According to Zeitschrift fur arztliche Fortbildung und Qualitatssicherung, Lavandula stoechas teas for insomnia cannot be recommended for treatment due to a lack of observed sleep inducing potential.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Lavandula stoechas teas can be used to treat insomnia, but it may cause side effects such as stomach upset, dizziness, and allergic reactions in some individuals, particularly if consumed in large quantities or combined with other medications. When using Lavandula stoechas teas to treat insomnia, it is essential to start with small doses, gradually increase as needed, and avoid consuming it before operating heavy machinery or driving, as it may impair cognitive function and reaction time.

preparations.herb_desc: Schisandra chinensis teas have been traditionally used to treat insomnia due to their sedative and calming properties that help to promote relaxation and improve sleep quality. The bioactive constituents present in these teas, such as schisandrins, lignans, and phenolic acids, contribute to their sedative effects by modulating the activity of neurotransmitters and reducing inflammation. By regulating the body's sleep-wake cycle and promoting a sense of calm, Schisandra chinensis teas help to alleviate symptoms of insomnia, including difficulty falling asleep and staying asleep. The benefits of using these teas to treat insomnia include improved sleep quality, reduced stress and anxiety, and enhanced overall well-being.
preparation_procedure: Gather 2 teaspoons of dried Schisandra chinensis, 1 cup of boiling water, a cup or mug, and a strainer or tea infuser.
Place the dried Schisandra chinensis in the strainer or tea infuser.
Pour boiling water over the Schisandra chinensis and let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into the cup or mug using the strainer or tea infuser.
Drink the tea 30 minutes before bedtime to help with insomnia. Repeat as needed.
preparations.preparation_study: According to Journal of ethnopharmacology, Schisandra chinensis teas for insomnia show potent sedative and hypnotic activity, increasing total sleeping time, promoting deep slow wave sleep, and reducing sleep latency, suggesting its therapeutic use for insomnia.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Schisandra chinensis teas can be used to treat insomnia, but its use may lead to side effects such as increased blood pressure, dizziness, and stomach upset in some individuals. To minimize potential risks, it is recommended to start with a low dose and gradually increase as needed, and to avoid consuming it before bedtime, especially if you have a history of high blood pressure or other health conditions.

preparations.herb_desc: Lemna minor teas, also known as duckweed, have been traditionally used to treat insomnia due to their sedative and anxiolytic properties. This herbal preparation helps to calm the mind and promote relaxation, allowing individuals to fall asleep faster and sleep more soundly. The bioactive constituents of Lemna minor, including flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, are responsible for its sedative effects, which help to regulate sleep patterns and reduce stress levels. By consuming Lemna minor teas, individuals can benefit from improved sleep quality, increased energy levels, and a reduced risk of sleep-related disorders.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh or dried Lemna minor leaves.
Use 1/4 cup of Lemna minor leaves and place them in a tea infuser or a small muslin bag.
Steep the Lemna minor leaves in 1 cup of boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup and discard the leaves. Add 1 tablespoon of honey to sweeten, if desired.
Drink the tea 30 minutes before bedtime to help with insomnia.
preparations.preparation_study: According to Disease-a-month : DM, Lemna minor teas are not mentioned in the study provided for their use in treating insomnia.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Lemna minor teas can be used to treat insomnia, but possible side effects may include dizziness, nausea, and stomach upset due to its high concentration of iridoids and alkaloids. When using Lemna minor teas to treat insomnia, precautions should be taken to avoid overconsumption, as excessive intake can lead to allergic reactions, interact with other medications, and exacerbate underlying health conditions.

preparation_study: According to Australian family physician, Valeriana officinalis teas for insomnia are associated with improvements in some sleep parameters, such as sleep latency and quality of sleep, although further trials are needed for confident recommendation.
intro_study: According to the study, teas for insomnia, which are often made from herbs, may have some benefits in improving the quantity and quality of sleep due to their influence on gamma-aminobutyric acid (GABA)-synthesizing and GABA-metabolizing enzymes.
